NSString.description: The fundamental immutable string class in Foundation framework, representing Unicode text with rich manipulation capabilities. NSString objects are created using @"literal" syntax or factory methods.

length.description: Returns the number of UTF-16 code units in the string.

stringByAppendingString:.description: Returns a new string made by appending the given string to the receiver.

substringWithRange:.description: Returns a string containing characters within the specified range.

isEqualToString:.description: Compares the receiver with the given string for Unicode equality.

stringWithFormat:.description: Creates a new string using a format string and arguments, similar to sprintf.

componentsSeparatedByString:.description: Splits the string into substrings at each occurrence of the separator.

NSArray.description: An ordered immutable collection of objects in Foundation framework, created using @[] literal syntax. Elements are accessed by zero-based index and must be Objective-C objects, not primitive types.

count.description: Returns the number of objects in the array.

objectAtIndex:.description: Returns the object at the specified index, raising NSRangeException if out of bounds.

containsObject:.description: Returns YES if the array contains an object equal to the given object.

enumerateObjectsUsingBlock:.description: Iterates over all objects in the array, executing the block for each element.

filteredArrayUsingPredicate:.description: Returns a new array containing only elements that match the given predicate.

NSDictionary.description: An immutable collection of key-value pairs in Foundation framework, created using @{} literal syntax. Keys must conform to NSCopying protocol and are typically NSString objects.

count.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs in the dictionary.

objectForKey:.description: Returns the value associated with the given key, or nil if not found.

allKeys.description: Returns an array containing all keys in the dictionary.

allValues.description: Returns an array containing all values in the dictionary.

enumerateKeysAndObjectsUsingBlock:.description: Iterates over all key-value pairs, executing the block for each entry.

NSNumber.description: An immutable object wrapper for C numeric types including int, float, double, and BOOL. Created using @() literal syntax or factory methods like numberWithInt: for boxing primitives.

integerValue.description: Returns the value as an NSInteger (platform-native signed integer).

floatValue.description: Returns the value as a float.

doubleValue.description: Returns the value as a double.

boolValue.description: Returns the value as a BOOL (YES or NO).

NSMutableArray.description: A mutable ordered collection that extends NSArray with methods for adding, removing, and replacing elements. Created using [NSMutableArray array] or [[NSMutableArray alloc] init] factory patterns.

addObject:.description: Appends an object to the end of the mutable array.

removeObjectAtIndex:.description: Removes the object at the specified index from the array.

insertObject:atIndex:.description: Inserts an object at the specified index, shifting subsequent elements.

removeAllObjects.description: Removes all objects from the mutable array, leaving it empty.

sortUsingComparator:.description: Sorts the array in place using the given comparison block.

NSMutableDictionary.description: A mutable collection of key-value pairs extending NSDictionary with methods for adding, updating, and removing entries. Commonly used for building dynamic configuration and state containers.

setObject:forKey:.description: Adds or replaces the value for the given key in the dictionary.

removeObjectForKey:.description: Removes the key-value pair for the specified key from the dictionary.

removeAllObjects.description: Removes all key-value pairs from the mutable dictionary.

NSLog.description: Logs a formatted message to the Apple System Log facility and standard error, prepending a timestamp and process information. Accepts printf-style format specifiers and %@ for objects.

dispatch_async.description: Submits a block for asynchronous execution on the specified Grand Central Dispatch queue and returns immediately. Essential for offloading work to background threads and updating UI on the main queue.

dispatch_queue_create.description: Creates a new dispatch queue with the specified label and attributes. Pass DISPATCH_QUEUE_SERIAL for serial execution or DISPATCH_QUEUE_CONCURRENT for concurrent task processing.

@selector.description: Compiler directive that returns a SEL type representing the specified method selector, used for target-action patterns, performSelector calls, and dynamic method invocation in Objective-C runtime.
